lookback_window_note: Retroactive fill for missed night. Window: April 22-25, 2026 inclusive. Post-April-25 developments excluded per retroactive date discipline.
context_flags_applied: April 25 EO 14398 DEI certification deadline effective TODAY. IBM $17M FCA settlement (April 10) is the enforcement template. All federal contractors must incorporate the new anti-DEI clause into active contracts by today. Second-wave DOJ actions may begin from April 25 onward. High-exposure F500 entities flagged in sector alert. No specific additional settlements confirmed in April 22-25 window beyond IBM (previously flagged).

Musk drops fraud claims against OpenAI on April 25 (Fortune/Bloomberg, confirmed same-day reporting). Case narrowed to two claims: unjust enrichment and breach of charitable trust. Fraud withdrawal is a new, dateable, material governance development — changes the scope of INT and ACC scoring for the trial period. Trial still opens April 27 but nature of remaining claims differs from prior scan.

SpaceX/xAI confidential S-1 filed with SEC April 1 for June 2026 Nasdaq IPO at $1.5-1.75T valuation. French prosecutors have separately alerted SEC and DOJ to potential IPO market manipulation (orchestrated deepfake controversy). SpaceX prospectus explicitly warns that Grok investigations may result in market bans. IPO regulatory risk cluster now formally documented in SEC filing.

South Sudan UNMISS vote: No confirmed vote as of April 25. Vote expected before April 30 mandate expiry. Prior resolution 2779 (May 2025) extended mandate to April 30, 2026. Council's April 2026 programme lists renewal as priority. UNMISS re-queued — vote not yet held at scan close.

DRC: UN MONUSCO mission chief James Swan arrived in Goma April 24 on first field visit. Xinhua/Globalsecurity both confirm April 25 briefing from Swan calling for ceasefire monitoring conditions. Ongoing fighting in South Kivu despite Montreux peace talks (April 13-17). 6.4M displaced + 4.3M returnees. Material new development: Swan's conditions for effective monitoring are a scoreable posture signal.

Danaher $172.5M class-action shareholder settlement announced April 23, 2026 — largest securities class action settlement in US District Court DC since 1996. Allegations: misleading statements about bioprocessing business outlook 2022-2023. Confirmed new material in window. Danaher is also Gen-2 cluster (76.4, rank 22 F500). Dual-trigger: methodology pattern + securities fraud settlement.

General Mills WARN Act investigation opened April 7 by Strauss Borrelli law firm. Plant closure April 6 in Saint Charles, Missouri — 163 jobs, pizza crust facility. WARN Act requires 60-day written notice; question of whether proper notice was given. Confirmed new material: second active compassion-relevant event for a Gen-2 cluster entity.

Eli Lilly filed Supreme Court petition March 21, 2026 seeking to overturn FCA qui tam whistleblower provisions as unconstitutional. Direct attack on federal fraud accountability mechanism. Confirmed material in window; April D&O Diary analysis published April 2026. Also: ICCR 2025 human rights impact assessment flagged absence of HRDD process at Lilly.

Booz Allen Hamilton: Treasury cancelled all 31 contracts ($21M total) in January 2026 over IRS tax data breach (employee Charles Edward Littlejohn leaked Trump tax records + 406K taxpayer records to NYT/ProPublica). Stock fell 10%+ on announcement. This is a significant governance/transparency failure not previously flagged in the pipeline. Booz Allen is F500 rank 128, composite 48.4, never assessed.

Sweden: Government proposed new 'good conduct' requirement for Aliens Act in April 2026 — vaguely defined criteria that could deny or revoke residence permits for behavior not illegal for Swedish citizens. Implementation expected June 2026. Countries ceiling candidate at 97.2; this event provides compassion-relevant event evidence for T1 elevation.

Finland: Government submitted migration reform package to Parliament April 16, 2026 — faster deportation enforcement, entry ban provisions, citizenship test requirement. Emergency asylum law extended through December 2026. Amnesty International has consistently flagged Finland's emergency border law as incompatible with EU law. Countries ceiling candidate at composite 100.

Amnesty International annual report published April 20, 2026 — 'predatory world order' framing; 144 countries documented. Sweden, Finland, and other Nordic countries cited for asylum policy rollbacks. This is sector-level evidence for the countries ceiling audit cluster.

Myanmar date-drift correction confirmed: The 7.7-magnitude earthquake occurred March 28, 2025 (not April 24, 2026 as the April 24 scanner incorrectly noted). The one-year anniversary reporting (HRW March 27, 2026) was likely the source of confusion. Myanmar's current status: junta continued airstrikes even during ceasefire period (92 airstrikes post-earthquake in 2025). 2026 situation is ongoing junta atrocities at 5-year high. April 24 composite of 3.1 (proposed) is the live working number. No new acute event in April 22-25 window.

Palantir: Congressional letter deadline expired April 24. EFF published April 25 analysis 'Palantir Has a Human Rights Policy — Its ICE Work Tells a Different Story,' documenting gap between stated values and surveillance operations. No confirmed ICE/DHS response to congressional letter retrieved in April 22-25 window — non-response is itself a scoreable outcome.

Trump Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigned April 20, 2026 amid abuse-of-power allegations including affair with subordinate, alcohol use, spousal harassment of DOL staff. Keith Sonderling now acting Labor Secretary. Directly affects the US country entity assessment context: executive branch institutional governance failure. Third Trump Cabinet member to leave under misconduct cloud.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.key_trigger: Musk drops fraud claims April 25 — narrows trial to unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust only; trial opens April 27
top_entities.news_summary: Elon Musk withdrew fraud allegations against OpenAI, Sam Altman, and Greg Brockman on April 25, 2026, one business day before trial was set to begin in Oakland federal court. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers agreed to 'streamline' the case, leaving only two of 26 original claims: unjust enrichment and breach of charitable trust. The fraud claim withdrawal materially changes the trial's evidentiary scope — Brockman diary evidence and Zilis texts remain in record but may bear differently on the narrowed claims. Musk still seeks $134B (directed to OpenAI's charitable arm) and removal of Altman and Brockman. Jury selection scheduled Monday April 27.
top_entities.rationale: April 25 fraud claim withdrawal is a new, dateable, compassion-relevant governance event — changes the legal theory under which OpenAI's founding mission abandonment is being adjudicated. Remaining claims (unjust enrichment, breach of charitable trust) directly implicate INT and ACC dimensions. Trial commencement is 2 days away — immediate assessment window. Applied April 24; new material today justifies re-queue.
top_entities.assessor_action: Score fraud claim withdrawal as governance narrowing event. Note: charitable-trust breach claim still directly scores mission-integrity dimension. Retrieve trial day-1 (April 27) proceedings before finalizing.

top_entities.name: Danaher
top_entities.key_trigger: Danaher $172.5M shareholder class-action settlement April 23 — largest DC federal court securities settlement since 1996; Gen-2 cluster (76.4)
top_entities.news_summary: On April 23, 2026, Danaher Corporation announced a $172.5 million settlement of a shareholder class-action lawsuit alleging the company made false and misleading statements about its bioprocessing business outlook in 2022-2023, as demand for COVID-era vaccine equipment collapsed. Shareholders alleged Danaher misrepresented the durability of demand for diagnostics and biopharmaceutical manufacturing equipment. Claims Journal confirmed settlement April 24. Danaher denies wrongdoing. Lawyers for shareholders called it the largest securities class action settlement in US District Court for DC since 1996. Preliminary settlement filed with federal court; requires judge approval.
top_entities.rationale: Double-trigger: (1) $172.5M shareholder settlement for misleading statements is a major corporate transparency failure directly affecting INT and ACC dimensions; (2) Danaher is a confirmed Gen-2 cluster entity (76.4, rank 22 F500) sharing uniform profile with American Express, Eli Lilly, Cummins, U.S. Bancorp, and Masimo. Published composite 76.4 is near-certain to be a methodology artifact. Never assessed. F500 importance 15 pts. Staleness 25 pts. Volatility: composite 76.4 is 3.6pts from 80-boundary — within the 3-pt volatility window.
top_entities.assessor_action: First baseline assessment. Score $172.5M securities fraud settlement as INT/ACC failure. Gen-2 forensic audit required: verify published 76.4 against subdimension evidence. Assess bioprocessing mission (healthcare diagnostics) for genuine positive SYS dimension values.
top_entities.gen2_pattern: 4x7+3.5x1 = 76.4, confirmed cluster at F500 ranks 16-25; dual-trigger: settlement + methodology

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.key_trigger: UNMISS mandate expires April 30 — vote not yet held; 5-day window remaining; ongoing hostilities at 2018-scale peak
top_entities.news_summary: UNMISS mandate expires April 30, 2026 (5 days from scan date). Security Council vote not confirmed as of April 25 scan close. Prior resolution 2779 (May 2025) set this deadline. April 2026 Council programme lists renewal as top priority. The April 17 Council briefing documented UNMISS as 'vital yet hampered' by budget cuts and freedom-of-movement restrictions. Active SSPDF-SPLA-IO hostilities remain at worst scale since 2018 R-ARCSS. Electoral support mandate — last renewal before December 2026 planned elections — adds political dimension to renewal terms. Australia and the US expressed concern at April 17 session about impediments to UNMISS operations.
top_entities.rationale: UNMISS mandate expiry in 5 days is the defining time-pressure event for this entity. Vote outcome (renewal with full mandate, renewal with modified mandate, or lapse) is the primary scoring driver for ACT and SYS dimensions. Re-queued third consecutive night as deferred pending vote. Composite 0 critical-band. Countries importance 12 pts.
top_entities.assessor_action: Check UN press.un.org for UNMISS vote result before scoring. If vote has occurred: score renewal terms against ACT/SYS dimensions. If not yet held: maintain deferral with 5-day urgency note.

top_entities.name: General Mills
top_entities.key_trigger: WARN Act violation investigation opened April 7 (163 workers, Saint Charles MO plant closure); racial bias class-action re-instated April 22 (Georgia federal court)
top_entities.news_summary: Two compassion-relevant events in the April 22-25 window: (1) Strauss Borrelli law firm opened a WARN Act investigation April 7 into General Mills' closure of its Saint Charles, Missouri pizza-crust plant, which will eliminate 163 jobs by June 8. Federal law requires 60-day written notice before mass layoffs — investigators are examining whether General Mills provided sufficient notice. (2) A Georgia federal judge on April 22 gave Black factory workers a final opportunity to re-file their proposed class-action alleging racist harassment at General Mills facilities. General Mills is a Gen-2 cluster entity (76.4, rank 16 F500) sharing the 4x7+3.5x1 profile with Becton Dickinson, Danaher, and others. Additional: Texas AG Paxton previously took action against General Mills for violations of Texas consumer law. General Mills suing Liberty Mutual/ACE over ultra-processed food insurance coverage — unrelated to worker welfare.
top_entities.rationale: Triple-trigger: (1) WARN Act potential violation — worker welfare, notice rights, EMP dimension; (2) racial harassment class-action re-instated — EQU/BND dimensions; (3) Gen-2 cluster candidate (76.4, rank 16). WARN Act investigation is directly compassion-relevant: affects 163 workers' right to timely notice before job loss. Race discrimination at facilities implicates institutional equity culture. Never assessed. F500 importance 15 pts. Staleness 25 pts.
top_entities.assessor_action: First baseline assessment. Score WARN Act potential violation (EMP), racial class-action status (EQU), and Gen-2 forensic audit (published 76.4 vs. evidence-based profile). Assess General Mills' consumer nutrition mission against documented labor record.

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.key_trigger: SpaceX S-1 IPO filing confirms regulatory risk language: Grok investigations may cause market bans; Paris prosecutors' SEC/DOJ alert documented in prospectus
top_entities.news_summary: SpaceX filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on April 1, 2026, for a June 2026 Nasdaq IPO targeting $1.5-1.75 trillion valuation. The prospectus explicitly warns investors that 'ongoing investigations into xAI's creation and dissemination of sexually abusive imagery may lead the company to lose access to certain markets.' This is the first time xAI's Grok regulatory exposure has been disclosed in a formal securities filing — documenting the CSAM/deepfake investigation cluster as a material risk. French prosecutors separately alerted SEC and DOJ to potential IPO market manipulation: allegation that the deepfake controversy was 'deliberately orchestrated' to boost company valuations ahead of the IPO. New Space Economy reported S-1 details April 23. Combined with the prior scan's Musk non-appearance before Paris prosecutors April 20, the IPO regulatory risk is now materially escalated.
top_entities.rationale: IPO prospectus risk disclosure (April 23 reporting, April 1 filing) formally documents the Grok harm-investigation cluster as a material financial risk — the first time it appears in a regulated SEC filing. This elevates the evidence quality for BND/EQU dimensions from press coverage to company-admitted regulatory risk. Floor dynamics apply (2.2 is near-minimum); escalating evidence cluster cannot be further expressed but must be documented. Last assessed April 21.
top_entities.assessor_action: Score IPO prospectus risk disclosure as evidence-quality upgrade: Grok harm now company-admitted material risk, not merely press-documented. Note floor dynamics limitation. Document SEC/DOJ investigation status as INT/SYS dimension anchor.

top_entities.name: Finland
top_entities.key_trigger: Parliament extended emergency asylum law through Dec 2026; April 16 deportation-speed reforms submitted; composite 100 is ceiling artifact (countries night 5 candidate)
top_entities.news_summary: The Finnish Government submitted to Parliament on April 16, 2026 a package of migration reform measures: faster enforcement of deportation decisions, pre-emptive entry bans for security risks, and introduction of a citizenship test (effective 2027). Parliament has extended the emergency asylum suspension law through December 31, 2026 — a law Amnesty International has called 'inconsistent with EU law' and a 'green light for violence and pushbacks at the border.' Amnesty's April 20, 2026 annual report documented Finland's rolling back of migration and asylum protections, compromising fairness of asylum proceedings and imposing discriminatory citizenship requirements. Finland holds composite 100 — the maximum score — in the countries index, a score shared with Denmark and the uniform top-cluster that has been documented as a ceiling artifact across four consecutive nights.
top_entities.rationale: First T1 elevation of Finland based on independent event evidence — not just countries ceiling methodology. Emergency asylum law extension through Dec 2026 (April 16 parliamentary submission) is a direct negative event affecting ACT and EQU dimensions. Combined with countries ceiling artifact (100 composite, likely 25+ point inflation vs. evidence), this is the countries Night 5 natural anchor. Last assessed April 18 (7 days ago, staleness 15 pts). Countries importance 12 pts. Composite 100 has zero room for positive volatility; negative evidence creates immediate downward pressure.
top_entities.assessor_action: First evidence-based assessment combining: (a) countries ceiling methodology audit for 100 composite; (b) emergency asylum law extension as active compassion-relevant event; (c) Amnesty April 2026 annual report country findings. Ceiling correction expected to be similar in magnitude to Iceland (-12.5) and Denmark/Sweden pending assessments.

top_entities.name: Sweden
top_entities.key_trigger: Government proposed vague 'good conduct' Aliens Act requirement risking residence permit revocation for non-illegal behavior; countries ceiling candidate at 97.2
top_entities.news_summary: Sweden's government proposed in April 2026 a 'good conduct' requirement to be reintroduced into the Aliens Act for residence permit assessment — using vaguely defined criteria that could deny or revoke residence permits based on behavior that is neither illegal nor punishable for Swedish citizens, creating discriminatory two-tier legal standards for migrants. The citizenship test requirement (8-year residency threshold vs. prior 5-year) is expected to take effect June 1, 2026. Additionally, Sweden is among the countries documented in Amnesty International's April 20, 2026 annual report for rolling back asylum protections. Sweden holds composite 97.2 in the countries ceiling cluster — five nights of confirmed ceiling corrections have established the pattern. Last assessed April 17 (8 days, staleness 15 pts).
top_entities.rationale: Promoted from T2 to T1 on independent event evidence: (1) 'good conduct' Aliens Act proposal creates discriminatory residence-permit standard (EQU/SYS); (2) Amnesty April 2026 report documents Sweden in broader Nordic asylum rollback; (3) countries ceiling Night 5 candidate. The two-tier legal standard for migrants is directly compassion-relevant — it is not merely tighter policy but a potentially arbitrary standard applied exclusively to foreign nationals. Last assessed April 17.
top_entities.assessor_action: Evidence-based assessment combining: (a) countries ceiling methodology audit for 97.2; (b) good conduct Aliens Act proposal as active EQU event; (c) Amnesty annual report Sweden-specific findings. Expected ceiling correction within Exemplary band.

top_entities.name: Booz Allen Hamilton
top_entities.key_trigger: Treasury cancelled all 31 contracts ($21M) January 2026 over IRS tax-record data breach; employee leaked 406K taxpayer records; stock fell 10%+
top_entities.news_summary: In January 2026, the US Treasury Department cancelled all 31 of its contracts with Booz Allen Hamilton ($4.8M annual, $21M total obligations) after a Booz Allen contractor, Charles Edward Littlejohn, deliberately leaked President Trump's tax records to the New York Times and 406,000 taxpayer records to ProPublica. Littlejohn pleaded guilty in October 2023 and was sentenced to 5 years (maximum). Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent framed the cancellation as response to an insider-threat incident. Booz Allen's stock fell more than 10%. Booz Allen argued the breach occurred on government systems, not Booz Allen systems. Separately, Booz Allen proactively scrapped DEI programs in response to Trump executive orders (breaking defense reporting, February 2025). April 25 EO 14398 certification deadline applies to Booz Allen as a major federal contractor.
top_entities.rationale: Major negative governance/transparency event (January 2026) not previously captured in the pipeline. Treasury contract cancellation over insider data breach directly affects INT and ACC dimensions: Booz Allen's information security governance failed at scale (406K taxpayers affected). The event also represents a regulatory enforcement action by a government client — the most direct form of institutional sanction. Never assessed. F500 importance 15 pts. Staleness 25 pts. Composite 48.4, 8.4pts from functional/developing boundary.
top_entities.assessor_action: First baseline assessment. Score Treasury contract cancellation (January 2026) as INT/ACC/SYS governance failure. Assess scope of insider-threat controls gap. Note Booz Allen's DEI program elimination as separate EQU-dimension signal. April 25 EO 14398 exposure is secondary — no additional enforcement action confirmed in window.

top_entities.name: Eli Lilly
top_entities.key_trigger: Supreme Court petition March 21 seeking to eliminate FCA whistleblower (qui tam) provisions as unconstitutional; Gen-2 cluster (76.4); ICCR HRDD gap finding
top_entities.news_summary: Eli Lilly filed a petition with the US Supreme Court on March 21, 2026, asking justices to declare the False Claims Act's qui tam whistleblower provisions unconstitutional — a sweeping attack on the primary federal mechanism for pharmaceutical fraud accountability. The challenge stems from a $183M Medicaid drug-pricing judgment. Lilly argues qui tam provisions violate Article II separation of powers. A D&O Diary analysis published April 2026 noted the writ raises the broadest constitutional question about private fraud enforcement in decades. Additionally, the ICCR's 2025 Human Rights Impact Assessment found Eli Lilly has not adopted a formal HRDD process, and an ICCR concern flagged Lilly's shift away from insulin toward GLP-1s as a profitability-over-access concern for insulin-dependent diabetics. Eli Lilly is a Gen-2 cluster entity (76.4, rank 19 F500).
top_entities.rationale: Triple-trigger: (1) SCOTUS petition to eliminate FCA whistleblower mechanism — INT/ACC failure: company actively working to remove accountability infrastructure; (2) Gen-2 cluster candidate (76.4, rank 19); (3) ICCR HRDD gap + insulin-access concern — SYS/EQU. Never assessed. F500 importance 15 pts. Staleness 25 pts.
top_entities.assessor_action: First baseline assessment. Score SCOTUS FCA petition as governance/transparency event — intent to eliminate accountability mechanism is scoreable regardless of outcome. Gen-2 forensic audit: verify 76.4 subdimension profile. Assess GLP-1 access and insulin continuity against healthcare mission.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
top_entities.key_trigger: MONUSCO chief Swan arrived Goma April 24, briefed April 25 on ceasefire monitoring conditions; ongoing fighting South Kivu despite Montreux talks; 6.4M displaced
top_entities.news_summary: James Swan, the new MONUSCO Special Representative of the UN Secretary-General, arrived in Goma on April 24, 2026, on his first eastern DRC field visit, and issued a briefing on April 25 calling for essential conditions for effective ceasefire monitoring: secure operating environment, end to offensive drone use, and cessation of GPS signal jamming. Despite Montreux peace talks (April 13-17) resulting in a ceasefire monitoring MOU, fighting continued on multiple front lines in South Kivu. Both sides accused each other of violations. Al Jazeera reported April 19 that DRC government and M23 committed to civilian protection — but on-the-ground compliance is unverified. Total displaced: 6.4M internally + 4.3M returnees. Food insecurity projected to affect 26.6M people (quarter of population) in 2026.
top_entities.rationale: April 25 MONUSCO briefing (same day as scan) is the most recent confirmed development in the DRC situation. Swan's conditions for effective monitoring represent a new baseline assessment by UN leadership directly relevant to SYS dimension. Ongoing ceasefire violations make any positive scoring of humanitarian protection premature. Previously confirmed at 4.4 (April 22 assessment); new material in window warrants reassessment check. Countries importance 12 pts.
top_entities.assessor_action: Reassessment check: has anything improved since April 22 confirmation (4.4)? Swan briefing conditions + ongoing South Kivu fighting suggest no improvement. Confirm or hold at 4.4. Update evidence base with Montreux MOU status and Swan's monitoring conditions framework.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.key_trigger: IRC April 22 report: aid down 37% in months 3-6 of ceasefire; 71K+ fatalities; 150K children requiring acute malnutrition treatment; composite 3.4pts from critical-band boundary
top_entities.news_summary: The International Rescue Committee's April 22 report documented continued deterioration in Gaza humanitarian conditions six months post-ceasefire: aid inflows declined 37% in January-March 2026 versus October-December 2025 (first ceasefire quarter). UN estimates nearly 150,000 children need treatment for acute malnutrition. The $4B 2026 Flash Appeal is severely underfunded. Oxfam and other groups report Israel is allowing only a fraction of needed humanitarian aid. Doctors Without Borders and other organizations had their Israeli work registrations expire in late 2025; some have since resolved status but operational restrictions persist. Israel continues to bar UNRWA from direct personnel/aid access since March 2025. OCHA April 2026 forecast lists Palestinian question as top Security Council priority. Composite 23.4 is 3.4pts from the critical-band boundary (20).
top_entities.rationale: IRC April 22 report quantifies continued deterioration — 37% aid decline in second ceasefire quarter is new, dateable, scoreable evidence. Band-change risk: 3.4pts from critical boundary (within 3-pt volatility trigger at 3.4 — borderline). Children acute malnutrition scale (150K) represents mass-casualty welfare harm directly affecting ACT and BND dimensions. Last assessed April 17 (8 days ago); staleness 5 pts.
top_entities.assessor_action: Score IRC deterioration report against current 23.4 composite. Assess whether declining aid access and child malnutrition scale warrants crossing the critical-band boundary. Apply band-change protocol if evidence supports move below 20.

top_entities.name: Cummins
top_entities.key_trigger: Gen-2 cluster (76.4, rank 20 F500); $1.675B EPA 'defeat device' settlement (Jan 2024) — largest Clean Air Act penalty in history; shareholder and customer lawsuits pending
top_entities.news_summary: Cummins agreed in January 2024 to pay $1.642 billion in civil penalties — the largest Clean Air Act civil penalty in US history — for installing illegal defeat devices on more than 600,000 RAM 2500 and 3500 pickup trucks (2013-2023). The settlement required recall of 85%+ of affected vehicles within three years. Pending: shareholder class-action lawsuits from the disclosure period; customer lawsuits over vehicle performance and fraud. In 2026, Cummins must conduct dynamometer testing on 2026-2028 Ram diesel engines. Cummins is a Gen-2 cluster entity (76.4, rank 20 F500) sharing the uniform 4x7+3.5x1 profile with entities now confirmed as methodology artifacts. The defeat device settlement — a deliberate emissions-fraud scheme affecting overburdened communities — is inconsistent with the established-band composite.
top_entities.rationale: The $1.675B defeat-device settlement is the largest Clean Air Act civil penalty in US history — a deliberate environmental fraud scheme. While the settlement dates to January 2024, it has not been assessed against Cummins' published composite of 76.4 (established band). Combined with Gen-2 cluster candidacy, this is dual-trigger: methodology artifact + major historical harm event not reflected in published score. 2026 ongoing recall and testing obligations make this a live compliance situation. Never assessed. F500 importance 15 pts. Staleness 25 pts.
top_entities.assessor_action: First baseline assessment. Score EPA defeat-device settlement against BND/EQU/SYS dimensions. Gen-2 forensic audit: the 76.4 uniform composite is inconsistent with a company that deliberately cheated emissions tests on 600K vehicles. Assess ongoing recall compliance posture.

t2_monitor.news_summary: Year 4 of civil war; 14M displaced; 21M facing hunger. Berlin conference pledged €1B in humanitarian aid. Amnesty April 2026 annual report documents Sudan. No single new material event in April 22-25 window beyond rolling pattern.

t2_monitor.name: U.S. Bancorp
t2_monitor.news_summary: Minnesota federal court dismissed Meta Pixel privacy suit against U.S. Bank April 7 (standing grounds). Prior: CFPB $37.5M fine (2022) for unauthorized account opening. No new major event in April 22-25 window. Gen-2 cluster candidate (76.4, rank 21 F500). Monitor alongside Cummins, Danaher, Eli Lilly for F500 Gen-2 cluster sprint.

t2_monitor.name: American Express
t2_monitor.news_summary: Whistleblower retaliation suit (Bloomberg Law, July 2025 denial of dismissal motion) ongoing. Prior DOJ $230M settlement for deceptive marketing. No new major event in April 22-25 window. Gen-2 cluster candidate (76.4, rank 18 F500). Monitor alongside other cluster entities.

rotation_backfill.name: New Zealand
rotation_backfill.note: Countries ceiling candidate at rank 7. Employment Relations Amendment Act 2026 changed dismissal protections for high earners. Minimum wage increase April 1, 2026 ($23.50 to $23.95). No major compassion-relevant event in window. Rotation backfill for staleness.

Integration policy updates 2026. Human rights defender support active. No new event in window. Rotation backfill: unassessed status with ceiling artifact likely.

rotation_backfill.name: Masimo Corporation
rotation_backfill.note: Gen-2 cluster candidate (76.4, rank 23 F500). DOJ/SEC investigations, CEO employment dispute. April 25 EO 14398 federal medical-device contractor exposure. Was T1 on April 24; no new event in April 22-25 window. Carried forward as rotation backfill — sprint entity.

rotation_backfill.name: Canada
rotation_backfill.note: Rank 11 countries, established band. UN Watch April 10 flag: democracies enabling rights violators to chair UN bodies — Canada named. No major domestic event in window. Staleness backfill: never assessed. Rotation priority.

rotation_backfill.name: Netherlands
rotation_backfill.note: Countries ceiling candidate at rank 8, prior correction proposed (April 23: Netherlands −21.5) but last_assessed remains null in rotation-state. Netherlands co-hosted fossil fuels transition conference. Grok Dutch court restriction on nude image feature (prior event). Rotation backfill: unassessed ceiling entity.

sector_alerts.sector: Fortune 500 — Generation-2 Cluster Sprint (Night 7 anchor: Danaher)
sector_alerts.alert: Danaher's $172.5M class-action settlement (April 23) provides the second independent real-world harm event to validate a Gen-2 cluster entity — following Becton Dickinson ($20M ethylene oxide verdict, April 24 assessment). The sprint cluster is: American Express (rank 18), Eli Lilly (rank 19), Cummins (rank 20), U.S. Bancorp (rank 21), Danaher (rank 22), Masimo (rank 23). All share the 76.4 uniform profile. All have confirmed compassion-relevant evidence independent of the Gen-2 pattern: Danaher (shareholder settlement), Eli Lilly (SCOTUS FCA petition), Cummins ($1.675B defeat-device settlement), American Express ($230M DOJ deceptive marketing), U.S. Bancorp (CFPB unauthorized accounts), Masimo (DOJ/SEC investigations). The sprint is overdue by Night 7. Assessor should treat this as a coordinated batch: all six entities can be assessed in one structured session using the forensic anchor protocol.

sector_alerts.sector: Countries — Ceiling Audit Night 5 (Finland and Denmark)
sector_alerts.alert: Finland and Denmark are the last two unassessed composite-100 countries in the top-10 cluster. Finland now has independent event evidence (emergency asylum law extension April 16; migration reform package April 16) supporting T1 elevation. Denmark has no fresh event news but is elevated to T1 alongside Finland for methodological consistency — both must be assessed together as the final composite-100 pair. Five nights of corrections average -17.7 points. Sweden (97.2) also elevated to T1 with the Aliens Act 'good conduct' proposal. Countries ceiling sweep for ranks 1-10 is now 7 of 10 entities assessed or in queue.

sector_alerts.sector: AI Labs — Musk v. Altman Trial Scope Narrowed April 25
sector_alerts.alert: Musk dropped fraud claims against OpenAI on April 25, one business day before trial opens. Remaining claims: unjust enrichment and breach of charitable trust. The fraud claim withdrawal changes the evidentiary weight of Brockman diary and Zilis texts — they remain in record but their role under unjust enrichment/charitable trust differs from their role under fraud. The trial scope change is material for INT and ACC dimension scoring: the charitable trust breach claim directly addresses OpenAI's founding mission abandonment. Jury selection Monday April 27. Assessor should retrieve day-1 proceedings before finalizing OpenAI score.

sector_alerts.sector: AI Labs — SpaceX IPO Regulatory Risk Cluster Formally Documented
sector_alerts.alert: SpaceX's S-1 filing (April 1, 2026) explicitly warns investors that Grok investigations may result in market bans — first time xAI's regulatory exposure has appeared in a regulated SEC filing. Paris prosecutors have separately alerted DOJ and SEC to potential IPO market manipulation allegations. The combination of S-1 disclosure + French prosecutor alert elevates the evidence quality for xAI/Grok scoring: the harm cluster is now company-admitted material risk, not merely press-documented. SpaceX acquired xAI in February 2026 at $1.25T valuation. IPO targeted June 2026.

sector_alerts.sector: US Federal Contractor DEI — April 25 Certification Effective
sector_alerts.alert: EO 14398 anti-DEI clause is effective in all federal contracts as of today. IBM $17M settlement (April 10) is the enforcement template. No new settlements or EEOC letters confirmed in April 22-25 window beyond IBM. Booz Allen Hamilton was flagged for the first time today with the January 2026 Treasury contract cancellation (IRS breach, $21M) — a separate but concurrent federal-contractor governance event. Defense contractors (Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon/RTX, General Dynamics) are confirmed to have proactively scrubbed DEI content and published compliance statements, reducing enforcement risk. Second-wave DOJ FCA actions may begin from April 25 onward.

Four critical-band humanitarian situations remain active: (1) South Sudan: UNMISS vote pending, 5-day window; (2) DRC: MONUSCO chief Swan April 25 briefing on ceasefire monitoring conditions; ongoing fighting South Kivu; (3) Israel/Gaza: IRC quantified 37% aid decline six months post-ceasefire; 150K children requiring malnutrition treatment; (4) Myanmar: junta atrocities at 5-year high; earthquake recovery (March 2025) hampered by junta obstruction. All four are confirmed T1 entities. No improvement signal in any of the four in April 22-25 window.

data_integrity_notes.issue: Myanmar earthquake date-drift CONFIRMED: The 7.7-magnitude earthquake occurred March 28, 2025 — not April 24, 2026 as incorrectly stated in the April 24 scanner's news_summary and acute-event flag. The April 24 scanner appears to have confused HRW's one-year anniversary reporting (March 27, 2026) with a new earthquake. Myanmar's T1 anchor should be updated in rotation-state: no acute earthquake event in April 22-25 window; junta atrocities ongoing at 5-year high per HRW World Report 2026.
data_integrity_notes.action_required: Remove 'special_flag: acute-event-earthquake-april-24' from rotation-state or scan context. Myanmar's active status remains valid on prior atrocity evidence. Notify assessor that earthquake humanitarian response scoring (if done April 24) may need to be re-examined for date accuracy.

Key new material vs prior scan: (1) Musk drops fraud claims April 25 [OpenAI]; (2) Danaher $172.5M settlement April 23 [new entity]; (3) General Mills WARN Act + racial class-action [new entity]; (4) Eli Lilly SCOTUS FCA petition [new entity]; (5) Booz Allen Hamilton Treasury cancellation [first pipeline capture]; (6) Finland asylum law extension [countries ceiling T1 elevation]; (7) Sweden Aliens Act good conduct proposal [countries ceiling T1 elevation]; (8) SpaceX S-1 risk disclosure for xAI/Grok [evidence quality upgrade]; (9) MONUSCO Swan April 25 briefing [DRC]; (10) EFF Palantir analysis April 25 [evidence quality upgrade]
